manifest.description: Interactive dashboard built from the user-provided analytical archive on Democratic rhetoric, omissions, irony, and historical comparison around the 2026 Iran war debate.

top_insights.title: The archive is multi-layered, not single-track
top_insights.detail: The source set blends core Democratic anti-war arguments, internal party splits, Jeffries-specific escalation, and counter-arguments built around omissions, irony, and historical comparison.

top_insights.title: Jeffries operates as a bridge figure
top_insights.detail: His rhetoric acknowledges Iran as a threat while repeatedly attacking Trump’s method, legality, evidentiary basis, and planning discipline.

top_insights.title: Omission analysis is the largest expansion vector
top_insights.detail: The omission files widen the frame from constitutional process into nuclear capability, proxy violence, human rights, cyber risk, hostage-taking, and narcotics trafficking.

top_insights.title: Irony lenses turn rhetoric into accountability tests
top_insights.detail: The fiscal and moral irony documents convert political talking points into consistency checks against spending records, inflation, homicide, fentanyl, and enforcement policy.

top_insights.title: The dataset already hints at a reusable schema
top_insights.detail: The materials are rich enough to normalize into figures, statements, claims, premises, evidence, omissions, contradictions, and timeline events for future cross-politician scaling.

timeline.title: Jeffries sets dual-track stance
timeline.detail: Calls Iran a sworn enemy while insisting Congress alone can authorize offensive action.

timeline.title: Operation Midnight Hammer response
timeline.detail: Jeffries argues Trump misled the country and risks a disastrous war.

timeline.title: IAEA access collapses
timeline.detail: Post-strike narrative shifts toward reconstitution risk and inspection blackout.

timeline.title: Jeffries backs Iranian protestors
timeline.detail: Internal regime brutality becomes explicit in the archive.

timeline.title: War Powers resolutions introduced
timeline.detail: Kaine/Paul in the Senate and Khanna/Massie in the House formalize constitutional pushback.

timeline.title: Pre-strike escalation warnings
timeline.detail: Jeffries warns against another failed foreign forever war as regional posture intensifies.

timeline.title: Operation Epic Fury begins
timeline.detail: U.S. and Israel strike Iran, triggering immediate claims around legality, plan failure, and risk expansion.

timeline.title: Casualties and counter-framing expand
timeline.detail: Minab strike casualties and first U.S. troop deaths intensify both moral and strategic arguments.

timeline.title: CNN and congressional pressure peak
timeline.detail: Jeffries escalates toward all-out-war framing while Democrats push justification and funding questions.

timeline.title: Briefing backlash and failed Senate vote
timeline.detail: Zero-evidence rhetoric, supplemental funding rejection, and the failed Senate War Powers vote crystallize the dispute.

system_model.purpose: Store officeholder identity, party, role, committee membership, and ideological profile.
Capture each public remark with date, venue, medium, and direct quote text.
Normalize what the statement asserts: illegality, no imminent threat, no plan, diplomacy, etc.
Record the logic chain used to support each claim.
Attach cited facts, polls, briefing descriptions, casualty figures, and comparison tables.
Track relevant facts alleged to be minimized or excluded from the public framing.
Map inconsistencies across time, across actors, or between rhetoric and cited evidence.
Link rhetoric to geopolitical events, briefings, votes, and escalatory moments.

lenses.title: Core Democratic Frame
lenses.summary: Maps the main anti-war structure: legality, imminence, lack of plan, public opposition, diplomacy, and domestic priorities.

lenses.title: Jeffries Trajectory
lenses.summary: Tracks a shift from hawkish threat recognition to intensive process, evidence, and contradiction language.

lenses.title: Omission Ledger
lenses.summary: Aggregates the counter-argument that public anti-war rhetoric leaves out nuclear, proxy, cyber, hostage, human-rights, and narcotics context.

lenses.title: Consistency Audit
lenses.summary: Uses fiscal and moral irony documents to test whether rhetorical standards are applied consistently across domestic and foreign issues.

phases.label: Demanding proof
phases.detail: Adopts “not a scintilla of evidence” language and presses for classified justification.

phases.label: Pre-strike warning
phases.detail: Frames escalation as another failed foreign forever war before the major strike sequence.

phases.label: Full opposition
phases.detail: After briefings, argues there is zero evidence of imminent threat and links war expansion to domestic cuts.

watchpoints: Need for evidence chains and timeline comparison

contradictions.title: Who drove the war sequence
contradictions.summary: Trump suggested he may have forced Israel’s hand, while Rubio framed the move as the U.S. reacting to an expected Israeli action.
contradictions.tension: If the trigger story changes, the implied legal and strategic rationale changes with it.

contradictions.title: The obliterated-program problem
contradictions.summary: Jeffries repeatedly argues that if Trump already said the nuclear program was obliterated, the same program cannot cleanly serve as the urgent basis for a new strike cycle.
contradictions.tension: Either the earlier destruction claim was overstated, or the new emergency case is overstated.

contradictions.title: Threat recognition versus war rejection
contradictions.summary: Jeffries calls Iran a sworn enemy and bad actor while also arguing the administration showed zero evidence for imminent threat and lawful escalation.
contradictions.tension: The profile is not pacifist; it is a split between accepting the danger and rejecting the legal-evidentiary pathway used to confront it.

contradictions.title: Anti-war front versus hawkish Democratic minority
contradictions.summary: A visible anti-war bloc pushed War Powers restrictions while a smaller Democratic hawkish lane rejected or diluted them.
contradictions.tension: The archive cannot be scaled honestly unless party messaging is modeled as internally fragmented rather than monolithic.

contradictions.title: Domestic-cost rhetoric versus spending record
contradictions.summary: Fiscal irony files argue that leaders condemning war costs as anti-worker previously backed massive spending programs that critics say worsened affordability.
contradictions.tension: This does not prove the anti-war case is false, but it creates a reusable consistency test for future issue tracking.

contradictions.title: Leadership condemnation versus left dissatisfaction
contradictions.summary: Left critics argue leadership rhetoric remained too delayed and procedural even after leadership publicly opposed the war path.
contradictions.tension: A future observatory needs to track not just party-vs-party conflict, but elite-vs-movement divergence within the same coalition.
